nameMeanings.name: Claiborne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Clabburn, possibly a variant of Cliburn , but the latter is a northwest English name whereas Clabburn is found mostly in Norfolk and the South East, so it is more probably from a lost place in that part of England, perhaps named with Old English clǣg ‘clay’ + burne ‘spring, stream’.
History: William Claiborne (c. 1600–77) was a founding colonist in VA. His descendant, William Charles Claiborne (1775–1817) was the first governor of LA.

variants.name: Clayborn
meanings: English: variant of   Claiborne  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Cliburn
meanings: English (Middlesex): habitational name from Cliburn (Westmorland), named from Old English clif ‘slope, bank’ + burna ‘stream’. Alternatively, from the rare Middle English personal name Clibern, of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Clayburn
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lincolnshire): variant of   Claiborne  .

nameMeanings.name: Rebecca
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from the Latin form of the Hebrew name Rebekah, borne by the wife of Isaac, who was the mother of Esau and Jacob (Genesis 24–27). The Hebrew root occurs in the Bible only in the vocabulary word marbek ‘cattle stall’, and its connection with the name is doubtful. In any case, Rebecca was Aramean, and the name probably has a source in Aramaic. It has always been common as a Jewish name; in England and elsewhere it began to be used also by Christians from the 14th century onwards and especially at the time of the Reformation, when Old Testament names became popular. It was very common among the Puritans in the 17th century, and has enjoyed a tremendous vogue in England since the latter part of the 20th century, among people of many different creeds. In Scotland this is found as an Anglicized form of Beathag .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Rebecca Claiborne was born in 1593, in Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas G. Claiborne, was 20 and her mother, Sarah Elizabeth Fenne, was 18. She married Sir Richard Coleman about 1613, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1650, in Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 57, and was buried in Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Rebecca Claiborneが1593年にイギリススフォルクで生まれた時，父Thomas G. Claiborneは20歳，母Sarah Elizabeth Fenneは18歳であった。約1613年にイギリスイギリスでSir Richard Colemanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1650年にイギリスイギリススフォルクブレント エレイグで57歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクブレント エレイグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Rebecca Claiborne(이)가 1593년 영국 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas G. Claiborne(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Elizabeth Fenne(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 대략 1613년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Richard Coleman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1650년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 브렌트 엘리에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 브렌트 엘리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Rebecca Claiborne родилась в 1593 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas G. Claiborne было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Elizabeth Fenne, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Coleman около 1613 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1650 г. в местности брент элиг, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 57 лет и была похоронена в местности брент элиг, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
